title: Aster arenosus
intro: Aster arenosus, also known as White Sagebrush Aster or Western Aster, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may aid in reducing inflammation, improving digestive health, and protecting against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Aster arenosus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Aster arenosus include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Aster arenosus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, which is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Aster, a group of plants that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. The genus Aster is further divided into several sections, and Aster arenosus is classified under one of these sections. Aster arenosus is a perennial herb, characterized by its daisy-like flowers and hairy foliage. Its classification is further detailed at the species level, where it is recognized as a distinct taxon within the Aster genus.
